courseleaf.title: Insect Biology, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The Department of Entomology & Nematology has six minor programs open to students in other disciplines seeking to complement their academic studies with focused training.

Minor Advisor

Lead Faculty Advisors

Shahid Saddique, Ph.D., Rachel L. Vannette, Ph.D., Vice Chair. 

Academic Advisor

Elvira Galvan Hack; 160 Hutchison Hall; 530-754-7277; Entomology & Nematology Advising.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Entomology
ENT 100General Entomology4
ENT 100LGeneral Entomology Laboratory 2
Choose at least 7 units:7-9
ENT 102
Insect Physiology
ENT 103
Insects Systematics
ENT 104
Behavioral Ecology of Insects
ENT 105
Insect Ecology
ENT 107
California Insect Diversity
ENT 109
Field Taxonomy & Ecology
Choose at least two additional upper division Entomology (ENT) courses (except 192, 198, 199) adding to at least 6 units.6-12
Upper division Entomology (ENT) courses.
Total Units19-27
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Insect Biology minor teaches students about the different types of insects and the traits that make them important and successful animals. Insects share many traits with other organisms, and so are widely used as model systems for understanding biology. Their adaptations also determine how they interact as pests, pollinators, recyclers of organic material, and vectors of pathogens, among other roles.
